title: Rank-aware race-group ensemble
summary: The metric is ROC AUC, so ordering positives above negatives matters more than calibrated probability.
rationale: The metric is ROC AUC, so ordering positives above negatives matters more than calibrated probability. Pit decisions are naturally compared within a race context: which driver is most likely to pit next on this lap or soon.
implementation_hint: Train an additional LightGBM/XGBoost ranking-style model by grouping rows at Race or Race-Lap level, using PitNextLap as relevance. If ranking objectives are awkward, approximate with per-race balanced sampling and AUC-focused hyperparameters: shallow trees, high min_child_samples, subsample by Race, and multiple seeds. Blend its rank-normalized predictions with the current best classifier.
expected_effect: Could improve AUC by optimizing relative ordering within the same strategic context, especially when global probabilities differ across years/races.
risk: Ranking group choice is delicate: Race-Lap groups may have too few positives, Race groups may be too broad. Raw ranker scores may need monotonic rescaling before blending.
